blocks.content: This tutorial walks you through configuring SAML authentication with OKTA as the Identity Provider in the Grafana UI. SAML is available in **Grafana Enterprise** (10.0+) and on **select Grafana Cloud plans**.

For more information refer to the [Configure SAML authentication using the Grafana user interface](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/saml-ui/)) and [Configure SAML with Okta](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/configure-saml-with-okta/) documentation

> - You need permission to read and update SAML settings in the UI (the **Authentication configuration writer** role / `fixed:authentication.config:writer`). 
      - Use a stack where you can change authentication settings. Read-only sandboxes (for example Grafana Play) are not suitable.
      - UI changes override SAML settings from the configuration file or environment variables.
You will confirm you can administer SAML in Grafana and Okta before changing either system.

In Okta, ensure you can open the **Admin Console** and create a SAML app integration. See Okta’s [Add app integration](https://help.okta.com/en/prod/Content/Topics/Apps/apps-overview-add-apps.htm) if needed.

In Grafana, confirm you have access to **Administration** > **Authentication and authorization** (or equivalent) for SAML. See [Roles and permissions](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/administration/roles-and-permissions/) if you are unsure.
You now know which permissions you need on both sides.

blocks.title: Open the SAML configuration in Grafana

blocks.content: You will open the SAML provider configuration so you can run the wizard and copy Service Provider URLs for Okta.

Go to **Administration** > **Authentication and authorization**, choose **SAML**, or open the SAML configuration page directly.

---
If your menu differs slightly, use the same destination: the SAML authentication settings for your stack.
blocks.tooltip: Skip only if your role cannot open SAML settings; otherwise open **Administration** > **Authentication** > **SAML** manually.

blocks.content: You are on the SAML settings page (or you know how to open it from the menu) and are ready to use the SAML wizard.

blocks.title: SAML UI wizard: General and Sign requests

blocks.content: You will complete **General settings** and **Sign requests** before connecting Okta.

In **General settings**, set options such as **Allow signup**, **Auto login**, **Single logout**, and **Identity provider initiated login** as needed. See the field table in the [SAML UI documentation](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/saml-ui/#general-settings). Select **Next: Sign requests**.

In **Sign requests**, choose whether outgoing requests are signed. If they are, provide or generate a certificate and private key (for example **Generate key and certificate** in the UI). Complete this section yourself—do not share private keys. Select **Next: Connect Grafana with Identity Provider**.
You have reached the step where Grafana exposes URLs and fields for the Identity Provider.
You will copy the **Metadata URL** and **Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL** from Grafana and use them in Okta.
In **Connect Grafana with Identity Provider**, find **Configure IdP using Grafana Metadata**. Copy the **Metadata URL**—you will give this to Okta so it can read Service Provider metadata.
Copy the **Assertion Consumer Service URL** (ACS). In Okta this is usually the **Single sign on URL** / Reply URL.
You have the SP URLs Okta needs. Keep this page available while you configure Okta.

blocks.title: Create and configure the SAML app in Okta

blocks.content: You will create a SAML 2.0 app in Okta and paste the URLs from Grafana.
Sign in to the [Okta portal](https://login.okta.com/), open **Admin Console** (switch to **Classic UI** from Developer Console if needed), then open **Applications**.
Choose **Create App Integration**, select **SAML 2.0**, then **Create**.
On **General Settings**, name the app (for example Grafana) and add a logo if you want.
On **Configure SAML**, set **Single sign on URL** to the **ACS URL** you copied from Grafana (`/saml/acs`).

Set **Audience URI (SP Entity ID)** to your Grafana metadata URL (typically the `/saml/metadata` URL shown in Grafana or derived from your instance). It must match the Service Provider **entity ID** Grafana expects.

Adjust **Name ID format** and **Application username** if you use SAML Single Logout; otherwise defaults are often enough. See [Configure SAML with Okta](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/configure-saml-with-okta/) for details.

Under **ATTRIBUTE STATEMENTS (REQUIRED)**, add attributes whose **names** match what you will enter in Grafana **User mapping** (for example **Login**, **Email**, **DisplayName** with Okta expressions such as `user.login`, `user.email`, and a display name). Names must align with the Grafana **Assertion attributes mappings** fields.
Optionally under **GROUP ATTRIBUTE STATEMENTS**, add a group attribute (for example **Group**) if you use group or team sync in Grafana.
Finish the Okta wizard (**Next**, then **Finish**). Note your Okta **Identity Provider metadata** URL or file—you will paste metadata into Grafana next.
Okta is configured to trust Grafana’s ACS and audience and to send the attributes Grafana will map.

blocks.title: Finish SAML in Grafana: IdP metadata and User mapping

blocks.content: You will paste Okta metadata into Grafana, map assertions, then test and enable.
Still under **Connect Grafana with Identity Provider**, paste **IdP metadata** into Grafana (URL, file path, or Base64—whichever the UI offers). This tells Grafana how to connect to Okta.

Select **Next: User mapping**. In **Assertion attributes mappings**, enter the attribute names that match Okta (for example Login, Email, DisplayName). See the [Okta attribute table](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/configure-saml-with-okta/) in the official guide.

If you use groups or role sync, complete **Groups attribute**, **Role mapping**, **Org mapping**, or **Allowed organizations** as needed. See [SAML UI](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/saml-ui/#user-mapping) and related topics (team sync, role sync, org mapping).
Select **Next: Test and enable**, then **Save and enable**. Fix any validation errors the UI shows, then use **Save and apply** if the UI prompts you after edits.
SAML is enabled from Grafana’s perspective and aligned with Okta’s metadata and attributes.
You will decide whether you need an extra assertion for SCIM-provisioned users.

If you use **SCIM** with Okta, align the SAML assertion with the provisioned user (for example an external ID claim). Follow the **SCIM** subsection in [Configure SAML with Okta](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/configure-saml-with-okta/).
You know when to add SCIM-specific mapping beyond basic Login, Email, and display name.

---

**Reference:** [SAML UI](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/saml-ui/) · [Configure SAML with Okta](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/configure-saml-with-okta/) · [SAML authentication in Grafana](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/) · [Okta guide source](https://github.com/grafana/grafana/blob/main/docs/sources/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/saml/configure-saml-with-okta/_index.md)
